checks.evidence: 2/4 providers passed the full graded live suite
2 partial (ran, missed a task), 0 blocked (no passing task), 0 missing diagnostics
every recorded capability artifact validates against capability_run_v1
pass reflects suite structure (hard tier present, 3 tiers {'easy': 4, 'medium': 3, 'hard': 3}); observed capability_score spread among graded providers 0.0 (did not separate them)

limitations: Capability readiness grades real code tasks; it is not a retired Fable 5 rerun.

A provider must pass every task in the graded suite to count as graded-live; a 'partial' provider ran the live suite and passed some but not all tasks (its real tasks_passed/capability_score are reported, e.g. mimo_code 9/10, hermes 8/10) and is evidence but not graded-live.
'blocked' is reserved for genuine non-execution (usage limit, headless lock, or no code emitted, i.e. zero passing tasks); it is not used for providers that ran and missed a task.

The graded suite grades against fixed public unit cases with no held-out or randomized inputs, so a capability_score certifies 'emitted working code on a fixed public set', not overfitting-resistant capability.
This is a cross-CLI harness comparison, not cross-model-family: claude_code routes to mimo-v2.5-pro, so distinct model backends are at most 3.
A zero capability_score_spread means the suite did not separate the providers at this difficulty, not that they are proven equivalent.
Remote CI and release publication remain separate evidence tracks.

Use it to document the expected shape for external baseline ingestion.

external_baseline.source: Example schema only. Replace with an authorized external Fable 5 run artifact before making comparative claims.

basis: capability matrix grades real code tasks with an isolated test harness
grading is objective (unit cases), not model self-grading
two graded-live providers with blocked diagnostics can support 98/100
three graded-live providers can support 99/100; four close the full matrix
